steps.title: Track the poem focus
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The speaker begins by saying that a song may be a little thing, but singing brings joy. The rest of the lines keep returning to the speaker's feelings: singing gives zest during toil, comfort at evening, and peace when the day ends.

steps.title: Separate mood from setting
steps.content: The cows, fold, bell, and stars create a rural evening scene, but that setting is not the point by itself. Those details show when and where the speaker sings, while the repeated emphasis stays on how singing changes the speaker's state of mind.

steps.title: Eliminate narrower readings
steps.content:
• Choice B misreads Night as a literal singing shepherd; the poem personifies Night and does not compare the speaker to a shepherd.
  • Choice C overfocuses on farming imagery, which supplies the setting rather than the main purpose.
  • Choice D is wrong because the speaker describes singing a song, not listening to pieces of music.
The answer is A.
